untranslatable_reason: This signal requires sentiment analysis and peer behavioral/emotional state tracking ('cold/uncertain' vs 'hot/confident' tone, hesitation detection, conviction shifts). These qualitative psychological metrics cannot be expressed using only technical price/volume indicators (RSI, MACD, ATR, etc.). The rule depends on: (1) NLP sentiment classification of peer communications, (2) temporal tracking of emotional state transitions, (3) directional bias reversal detection from non-price sources. No allowed indicators can proxy for peer sentiment, communication tone, or psychological conviction shifts.
translation_notes: Signal originates from Jack Schwager's description of contrarian trading based on reading trader psychology ('cold as ice'). While the concept is sound (contrarian positioning against uncertain/hesitant traders), it requires external data feeds (chat logs, call transcripts, social sentiment) and NLP classification layers not present in a standard technical backtest engine. To make this backtestable, you would need: (1) a peer sentiment score input, (2) conviction/heat proxy metrics, (3) bias reversal confirmation signals from external sources, which fall outside the technical indicator universe provided.
